ncs_standard_definition.description_short: Designs and develops plans for the physical development of cities and towns, ensuring efficient use of resources and infrastructure.
ncs_standard_definition.description_detailed: City Planners analyze data, conduct research, and collaborate with stakeholders to create comprehensive plans for urban development, transportation systems, and public spaces, balancing economic, social, and environmental considerations.

first_stepper.description: High school students interested in urban planning can start by taking courses in geography, economics, and mathematics.

choice_maker.description: Individuals with a generic degree can pursue certifications in urban planning, such as the Certified Urban Planner (CUP) or the American Institute of Certified Planners (AICP) certification.

future_builder.description: Experienced city planners can advance their careers by developing expertise in specialized areas, such as transportation planning, environmental planning, or urban design.
future_builder.prerequisite_experience: 5 years as a Junior Urban Planner

problem_solver_uturn.description: Individuals from unrelated fields can transition into city planning by acquiring relevant skills, such as data analysis, spatial reasoning, and communication.
